title: Source lineage for The Petworth MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
summary: The pinned CME XML is a normalized copy of U-M's 2006 ASH2689.0001.001 encoding, which encodes Furnivall's seven-part 1868-1884 manuscript-specific edition. The edition continuously transcribes the Petworth/Leconfield Chaucer except for explicitly bounded supplies from six other manuscripts. Comparison witnesses, the Six-Text context, consulted editions, announced visual sources, physical carriers, digital packages, and provider surrogates are separately classified. Rights remain access- and entity-specific: the repository Unlicense and historical U.S. public-domain conclusions do not transfer to modern encoding, page images, physical manuscripts, or provider photography.
review_notes: Keep textual work, physical witness, scholarly edition, print carrier, electronic encoding, digital surrogate, preservation package, and repository artifact distinct.
The primary path contains only the strongest direct derivation chain; supplies, comparisons, consulted editions, announced visuals, and facsimiles are classified once in supporting groups.
A challenged route is not absent; a provider selection is not a complete manuscript; a partial scan is not a complete edition.
Public-domain conclusions for historical text do not transfer to physical objects, modern photography, provider metadata, OCR/TEI, or repository licensing.
Unresolved date, foliation, issue-state, source-locus, and rights conflicts remain explicit open questions.

conflict:edition-date-span: U-M header/title data describe London 1868-1879 versus the seven physical parts are cataloged as 1868-1884; disposition: retain both; use 1868-1884 for issue span and 1868-1879 only as the bound title span until issue-state research is complete.

conflict:menagier-date: ASH XML says Le Ménagier, ed. 1847, volume i, p. 186 versus the cached volume's title page and Commons metadata say 1846; disposition: identify the cached edition as 1846 and preserve the XML's 1847 citation as an unresolved bibliographic conflict.

conflict:franklin-second-bracket-lines: adjacent anchors imply F1432-1433 versus the alternative transition implies F1433-1434; disposition: preserve exact token position, Royal 17 D XV f. 188a, and edition p. 396 without forcing line numbers.

conflict:ellesmere-folio-labels: scholarly folio labels map the 23 portraits to the recorded folio/canvas pairs versus the provider inventory labels one side earlier; disposition: retain the scholarly folio plus provider-canvas crosswalk and flag the provider-label offset for review.

conflict:illustration-carrier-state: the edition advertises 23 Ellesmere portraits and nine Gg woodcuts/illustrations versus the U-M carrier is cataloged as wanting nine plates and nine Gg woodcuts, perhaps never issued; disposition: model Ellesmere and Gg as announced visual sources, not as images proven present in this carrier.

entities.label: The Petworth MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
entities.description: Furnivall's manuscript-specific seven-part Petworth/Leconfield one-text edition represented by the U-M carrier. Excludes the abstract Canterbury Tales work; the Petworth physical manuscript as a whole; a homogeneous Petworth-only transcription; the U-M encoding, page images, and repository artifact.
notes: Boundary exclusions: the abstract Canterbury Tales work; the Petworth physical manuscript as a whole; a homogeneous Petworth-only transcription; the U-M encoding, page images, and repository artifact.

entities.description: Furnivall's manuscript-specific seven-part edition: a continuous Petworth/Leconfield base transcription with six separately bounded supply witnesses, comparison witnesses, consulted editions, and an announced illustration program kept distinct in the relations.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Pinned CME artifact to principal Petworth witness
primary_transmission_paths.description: The strongest direct chain: pinned CME XML copied from the U-M encoding, encoded from Furnivall's edition, whose continuous principal transcription is Petworth/Leconfield except at separately modeled supply loci.

supporting_relationships.label: Edition-level catalog boundary
supporting_relationships.description: Keeps the selected catalog object at the Furnivall edition level rather than conflating it with the abstract Canterbury Tales or the Petworth codex. Packet classification: supporting_boundary.

supporting_relationships.label: Print carrier and page surrogate
supporting_relationships.description: Connects the exact U-M source copy to the historical edition and its 758-image page surrogate. Packet classification: supporting_carrier.

supporting_relationships.label: Raw-package containment
supporting_relationships.description: Records that the complete U-M preservation package contains both the raw encoding files and the full TIFF page sequence. Packet classification: supporting_digital.

supporting_relationships.label: Bounded direct supply witnesses
supporting_relationships.description: Six manuscripts supply explicitly bounded passages within the otherwise Petworth-based edition. Packet classification: supporting_direct_supply.

supporting_relationships.label: Comparison and collation witnesses
supporting_relationships.description: Five witnesses are used for absence, correction, or paragraph comparison, not as continuous base text. Packet classification: supporting_comparison.

supporting_relationships.label: Six-Text contextual witnesses
supporting_relationships.description: Two witnesses are represented through the consulted parallel-text edition and are not asserted as direct ASH transcription sources. Packet classification: supporting_six_text_context.

supporting_relationships.label: Consulted editions
supporting_relationships.description: Separates navigation, paragraphing, and translated-source consultation from manuscript transcription. Packet classification: supporting_consulted_edition.

supporting_relationships.label: Announced visual sources
supporting_relationships.description: Records the Ellesmere portraits and Gg illustrations announced for the edition without claiming they survive in the U-M carrier. Packet classification: supporting_announced_visual.

supporting_relationships.label: Digital surrogates
supporting_relationships.description: Links complete, selected, volume-level, and partial digital carriers to the witnesses or editions they reproduce. Packet classification: supporting_surrogate.

rights.component: repository artifact
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository artifact is distributed under the repository Unlicense.
notes: This grant is not projected onto U-M data, page images, historical edition typography, manuscripts, or modern photographs.

rights.component: historical edition text and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: The 1868-1884 edition is in the U.S. public domain by publication date.

rights.component: physical manuscript object, consultation, and any future photography
rights.jurisdiction: National Trust access and reproduction terms
rights.basis: The catalog establishes the physical object's identity and discovery route only. No blanket textual public-domain conclusion, physical access grant, or permission for existing or future photography is attached to the witness entity.

rights.component: any materially unpublished manuscript content not established as published by the edition
rights.basis: Residual U.K. unpublished-work rules may require a work-by-work review through 2039; no blanket conclusion is made for the entire physical witness.

rights.component: 2006 XML/OCR encoding
rights.basis: The cached U-M/Internet Archive package supplies no explicit item-level reuse license for the modern encoding.

rights.component: 758 page-image files
rights.basis: Internet Archive access and age of the printed book do not themselves establish a license for the U-M page-image files.

rights.component: preservation package delivery
rights.basis: The noindex Internet Archive item makes provider files retrievable but does not state a universal reuse license.

rights.component: modern selected photographs
rights.basis: The selected images are modern National Trust photographs; the first record credits National Trust / Andrew Fetherston and offers an orderable master. No open reuse license was found.

rights.component: modern manuscript images
rights.basis: Embedded manifest: © Governing Body Christ Church; all rights reserved; reproduction permission from the Librarian.

rights.component: modern selected manuscript images
rights.basis: The embedded Digital Bodleian manifest applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the selected images.

rights.component: modern manuscript images
rights.basis: Provider permits noncommercial use with attribution; commercial use, including academic publications, requires permission.

rights.component: modern manuscript images
rights.basis: CUDL applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to images; metadata has a separate CC BY-NC-ND statement.

rights.component: modern manuscript images
rights.basis: The embedded manifest states no open license and points to Huntington rights and permissions guidance.

rights.component: digital surrogate images
rights.basis: The embedded NLW manifest identifies the digital surrogate with the Public Domain Mark 1.0.

rights.component: historical edition
rights.basis: The 1775-1778 edition is in the U.S. public domain by publication date; Internet Archive delivery is kept as a separate access fact.

rights.component: historical edition
rights.basis: The nineteenth-century Six-Text parts are in the U.S. public domain; the cached carrier remains explicitly partial.

rights.component: historical edition scan
rights.jurisdiction: Wikimedia Commons provider assertion / United States
rights.basis: Commons labels the delivered 1846 volume scan public domain. This is the provider's scan-layer assertion; the historical edition has a separate rights row.

rights.component: historical textual and editorial selection as published in the 1868-1884 Furnivall edition
rights.basis: The selected textual/editorial work as published in 1868-1884 is in the U.S. public domain. This does not determine rights in physical witnesses, modern photographs, the U-M encoding, or provider delivery files.

rights.component: physical printed copy, ownership, and physical consultation
rights.basis: The cached surrogate proves the existence and pagination of the digitized source copy, but it does not establish current ownership, physical consultation terms, or permission to reproduce the carrier as an object.

rights.component: physical manuscript object, consultation, and any future institution-supplied photography

editorial_practices.description: The cataloged object is Furnivall's manuscript-specific seven-part edition: 24 conventional Chaucerian tales counted once with links/frame, a separate anonymous Gamelyn appendix, textual appendices, and apparatus.
editorial_practices.scope: The entire catalog boundary; it is not the abstract Canterbury Tales or the Petworth codex as a physical whole.

editorial_practices.description: Petworth is the continuous principal transcription, while six manuscripts supply bounded passages and five further witnesses support comparison or correction.
editorial_practices.scope: Only the exact passage, folio, page, and role scopes recorded on the relations; no supporting witness is promoted to a homogeneous base text.

open_questions.question: Can a complete Petworth/Leconfield facsimile or scholarly consultation be obtained?
next_steps: Contact Petworth House/National Trust and request research access or a complete folio-level surrogate.

open_questions.question: What exact folios appear in selected image 1 and in the recto/verso final-leaf views 3-4?
next_steps: Obtain provider master metadata or compare directly against a complete manuscript foliation.

open_questions.question: Which Barlow 20 folio supplies F679-680?
next_steps: Consult a complete facsimile, request targeted imaging, or obtain a folio concordance; the cached f. 5r/f. 44r labels do not resolve target presence.

open_questions.question: How do the 1868-1879 bound title span and 1868-1884 seven-part issue span map to this U-M copy?
next_steps: Collate wrappers, title leaves, and bibliographic descriptions for each part against the 758-image carrier.

open_questions.question: Were the nine missing plates and nine Gg woodcuts ever issued with this exact copy/state?
next_steps: Compare other bound sets and Chaucer Society issue records before asserting presence or absence at edition level.

open_questions.question: Which conventional line numbers belong to the second Royal 17 two-line bracket on p. 396?
next_steps: Resolve from a diplomatic line-by-line comparison while preserving the current exact token-position locator.

open_questions.question: Why does the XML cite 1847 while the cached Le Ménagier volume is dated 1846?
next_steps: Check Furnivall's bibliography and alternate issue/title-page states; do not silently normalize the conflict.

open_questions.question: Which exact Tyrwhitt impression or reissue did Furnivall consult?
next_steps: Compare typography and paragraph breaks against the cached volumes and Furnivall's bibliographic wording.

open_questions.question: Can a complete carrier covering every XML Six-Text navigation milestone be pinned?
next_steps: Locate and fix a complete Six-Text set; retain the current IA PDF as Parts I-II only.

open_questions.question: Can every British Library supply/comparison locus be checked against current images?
next_steps: Order targeted imaging or consult the manuscripts; current exact catalog records establish identity but not visual verification.

open_questions.question: Why does the provider inventory label one side earlier than the scholarly Ellesmere foliation?
next_steps: Reconcile flyleaves/provider canvas labels with the standard manuscript foliation before manifest integration.

open_questions.question: What exact item-level reuse terms govern the U-M OCR/TIFF package and National Trust selected images?
next_steps: Request written terms from U-M and the National Trust; keep repository Unlicense and historical-text public-domain conclusions separate.

open_questions.question: How should the edition's legacy 'Petworth MS 7' designation crosswalk to current NT 3242251?
next_steps: Confirm the historic shelfmark in Petworth/National Trust catalog history before adding an identifier alias.
